english.headline: The Heartbeat: Alek's All-Action Day in Orelia
english.commentator: Hammond Park made the trip to Orelia and came home with a massive 103 to 26 win over Kwinana, and number 13 was the heartbeat of that performance from the first bounce. Alek played midfielder, forward, and defender across four quarters and brought the same thing to every single one of them — pure energy and a desire to be in every contest. In the first quarter alone he won six tackles from seven attempts in the middle of the field, shutting down the opposition and winning the ball back for his team again and again. That fire did not fade as he moved around the ground — Q3 saw him push forward in the second half of the quarter to help establish field control, reading the game exactly when his team needed it. When Alek plays with that kind of enjoyment, everything clicks — and today you could see it in every run, every chase, every contest he put himself into. Next up, Kelmscott at Frankland Park on the 4th of July — back on home turf and this team is flying.
